text: Databases are essential for storing and organizing information. Two main types exist: SQL and NoSQL. SQL databases, like MySQL, use tables with rows and columns, similar to spreadsheets. They require a strict structure, which is called data modeling. You retrieve data using queries written in SQL, a powerful language. To speed up searches, you can add indexes, which work like a book’s index. NoSQL databases, like MongoDB, are more flexible. They store data in documents or graphs, not tables. This makes them ideal for large, changing datasets. While SQL excels at complex relationships, NoSQL handles rapid growth well. Choosing the right type depends on your project’s needs: structure or scalability.
